input.background: We wish to estimate the mean weight of a population in which body weight has a log-Normal distribution with $\sigma=40$ pounds. Three independent SRS each of $n=712$ are selected from the population.
input.question: Calculate $95 \%$ confidence intervals for $\mu$ based on the second sample with $\bar{x}_2=180$.

output.answer: For sample 2, the confidence interval is $\bar{x}_2 \pm m=180 \pm 3=177$ to 183 (pounds).

sigma.description: Standard deviation of the log-Normal distribution of body weight in the population.

n.description: Sample size for each of the three independent simple random samples (SRS).

x_bar_2.description: Sample mean of the second sample.

confidence_level.description: Confidence level for the interval estimation of the population mean.

meta_info.caption: 95 % confidence intervals for $\mu, \sigma$ known (Body weight, 20-29-year-old males).
meta_info.section: 10.2 Confidence Intervals for $\\mu$ When $\\sigma$ Is Known
